implemented: Three-file architecture: lionscliapp bootloader, tkinter UI framing, copyable main module.
Tokenizer registry and per-cycle tokenizer phase.
RAW/DERIVED snapshots for the initial event surface.
Derived interaction facts for pointer delta, drag lifecycle, hit testing, and empty-space detection.
Minimal judge with resource holds and pointer exclusivity.
Dict-based organisms for focus, mode key, node creation, node click selection, single-node drag, marquee/group selection, group drag, edge creation, empty click clear, and delete.
Real effect queue with explicit apply step.
Rendering for nodes, selection highlight, marquee preview, edge preview, and existing edges.
tkintertester coverage for the initial slice.
deferred: More sophisticated judge maintenance and richer resource protocols.
Incremental organism-by-organism migration from RAW/interaction consumption to DERIVED consumption.
notes: Judge is intentionally minimal and rewriteable.
Organisms are the long-lived structure; the judge currently only models what the first slice needs.
Tokenizer introduction is intentionally incremental; node-drag is the first organism moved to DERIVED.
